quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 6
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A test projectile launched straight upward from ground level reaches a peak vertical displacement height of $84.5\text{ m}$ before falling back down. Calculate the initial launch velocity of the projectile, assuming negligible air resistance. ($g = 9.80\text{ m/s}^2$)

A high-precision CNC milling machine requires its daily travel limits to be programmed in small metric increments. Convert a recorded linear travel distance of $0.00315\text{ km}$ directly into millimeters ($\text{mm}$).

A metal block is tied to a string and swung in a vertical circle. As the block passes through the highest point of its loop, the string snaps, and the block enters free-fall. During the subsequent downward flight of the block toward the ground, state whether the work done by the gravitational force on the block is positive, negative, or zero.
questions.explanation: Gravity acts downward and the block displaces downward. Force and displacement are in the same direction — work is positive.

questions.question: An industrial mechanical press utilizes a heavy internal flywheel. The flywheel possesses exactly $6,300\text{ J}$ of kinetic energy at operational speed. Calculate the mass of a linear object that would possess this exact same amount of kinetic energy when traveling at $14.2\text{ m/s}$.

An automated testing rig applies a variable resistive force to a sliding platform over $4.80\text{ m}$. The platform's kinetic energy decreases from $1,240\text{ J}$ to $340\text{ J}$ during this test segment. Determine the magnitude of the average net force opposing the motion.

A high-speed camera records a golf club hitting a stationary $0.0460\text{ kg}$ golf ball. The ball flies off the tee at $64.0\text{ m/s}$. If the contact time is $0.00115\text{ s}$, calculate the average force exerted on the ball during impact.

A structural engineer estimates the performance requirements for an industrial conveyor belt that must transport mining aggregate. The conveyor moves the material horizontally at $2.25\text{ m/s}$ against a total friction resistance force of $3,600\text{ N}$. What is the minimum power output required from the drive motor?

A thermal power plant generator receives $8.40 \times 10^7\text{ J}$ of heat energy and exports $2.94 \times 10^7\text{ J}$ of useful electrical energy. Calculate the waste thermal energy dissipated and the overall efficiency percentage.

A $4.50\text{ kg}$ laboratory cart moving east at $3.20\text{ m/s}$ collides with a $1.50\text{ kg}$ cart moving west at $5.80\text{ m/s}$. After the collision, the $4.50\text{ kg}$ cart rebounds west at $0.200\text{ m/s}$. Calculate the final velocity of the $1.50\text{ kg}$ cart.

A maintenance garage utilizes a screw jack to elevate a vehicle chassis. The screw thread has a pitch of $0.450\text{ cm}$ and the jack handle has a rotational radius of $32.0\text{ cm}$. Determine the ideal mechanical advantage (IMA) of this screw jack configuration.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on work-energy theorem, impulse during rebounds, and screw jack IMA.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
